concept: Latrine / Place or Fixture for Bodily Waste
description: A place or fixture arranged to receive bodily waste. Designs include pits, composting systems, and water-carried fixtures. The noun identifies the daily place without claiming how treatment works. Exact sanitation instructions and infrastructure keep their source form when the details matter.
sound_symbolism: Closed lips give 'mu' a deep nasal opening. Stressed 'ne' moves the contact to the teeth and balances the center in 'e'. Final 'ki' closes farther back and rises to bright 'i'. From a low chamber, the sound climbs through two increasingly crisp points.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'ha womu muneki phelu.' (This home has a latrine.) Nearby clauses identify location, access, water use, maintenance, and sanitation method. The term covers a practical class rather than one technical fixture.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Sanitation belongs in material-cycle discussion because water and nutrients meet pathogens, labor, and access at this ordinary household place.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Pit placement and dry cover carried practical knowledge about keeping household waste away from drinking water. Cleaning routines carried the rest.

tags.community: a sanitation place used within shared or solitary daily life
tags.physical: a fixture or place receiving bodily waste
tags.spatial: a bounded sanitation area in or near a dwelling
